pi_items.source: Web search of US homeschool requirements across multiple states (PA, NC, MO, NY, OH, GA) shows no state specifies this exact breakdown. PA requires total hours (900-990) not per-subject. MO requires 1000 hours total with 600 in core (~120/subject average). This specific breakdown (Math 120, Lang Arts 160, Science 100, Social Studies 100, PE 60, Arts 40) does not match any real state requirement.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic homeschool compliance validation task with 81 lesson entries spanning September 3, 2024 to May 1, 2025. The task requires analyzing data across 6 subjects with state-mandated minimums: Mathematics (120 hrs), Language Arts (160 hrs), Science (100 hrs), Social Studies (100 hrs), Physical Education (60 hrs), and Arts (40 hrs) for a total of 580 hours required. Generated test data shows 3 compliant subjects (Math +5.5 hrs, Language Arts +8.0 hrs, Social Studies +3.0 hrs) and 3 deficient subjects (Science -4.5 hrs, PE -3.0 hrs, Arts -1.5 hrs). The task is medium difficulty (240-second timeout, 20 max steps) and requires SUMIF formulas to calculate subject totals, IF formulas for status indicators, and conditional formatting. Success requires passing 4 of 5 criteria (80% threshold). All data is synthetic, procedurally generated from predefined lists of lesson descriptions with controlled random variation. NOTE: The state requirements (Math 120, Lang Arts 160, etc.) do not match any actual US state homeschooling requirements found in web searches - this appears to be a fictional requirement set created for task design purposes.
